name: Fisetin vascular-function trial is active with no posted results
summary: The active older-adult fisetin vascular-function registry is high-value trial-watch context, but no efficacy or safety result should be inferred before data are posted.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT06133634 as an active-not-recruiting phase 1/2 randomized triple-masked fisetin trial in older adults with estimated enrollment of 70, endothelial-function primary endpoint at one month, estimated primary completion March 31, 2026, and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Older adults in a fisetin vascular-function trial
time_horizon: One-month endothelial-function primary endpoint with estimated study completion March 31, 2027
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 70; randomized allocation; triple masking; primary outcome is change from baseline in endothelial function at one month.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of benefit or harm.
Endothelial function is an aging-relevant endpoint but does not alone establish broad geroprotection.
Fisetin-specific tissue target engagement remains unresolved.
